channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Genshin Impact Version Luna VII Set to Arrive Later This Month
item.content:encoded:
HoYoverse has revealed the release date for the next major Genshin Impact update, with Version Luna VII set to bring new characters, new story elements, and more later this month.

As you can probably guess, Version Luna VII follows on from April's Version Luna VI, and while that update took you back to the land of Mondstadt, you'll be in Sumeru to continue the last Version's Archon Quest this time around.

To help you in your endeavors, you'll be able to recruit three new characters, starting with the 5-star Pyro Catalyst user Nicole, a mage who grants powerful support to your team and who can use her powers to search for treasure while you're exploring as well.

Nicole will be joined in the 5-star stakes by Lohen, a polearm-wielding Cryo user who deals big damage thanks to two unique combat resources. Finally, there's Prune, a 4-star Anemo Catalyst user who "uses a bell and a giant hammer to deal multi-Elemental DMG and trigger Swirl reactions".

In terms of reruns, the above characters will be joined by a rerun for Durin in the first half, followed by Mavuika in the second. If you missed your first chance to grab these characters, this should give you another opportunity to snap them up.

Version Luna VII will also bring with it a brand new limited-time event that will let you participate in "a variety of fun minigames" offering lots of different rewards, including a new unique weapon appearance.

As usual, HoYoverse has also revealed a few codes you can use to get some free stuff, so here are this update cycle's codes!

  • BacktoSumeru: 100 Primogems, 10 Mystic Enhancement Ore
  • NicolaCoco: 100 Primogems, 5 Hero's Wit
  • WilltoWin: 100 Primogems, 50,000 Mora

Genshin Impact is available to play for free right now on PC, PlayStation, Xbox Series X|S, and mobile devices. Version Luna VII launches across all devices on May 20th.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/genshin-impact-version-luna-vii-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 2K Lays Off Staff at Project Ethos Dev 31st Union
item.content:encoded:
2K has laid off a number of employees at 31st Union, the studio currently working on free-to-play roguelike shooter Project Ethos, but studio head Ben Brinkman reckons things are still on track.

In an internal memo shared with Gamesindustry.biz by 2K itself, Brinkman says that while he's "never been more confident in the future of [Project Ethos]", staff cuts are necessary in order to "better align" with the current state of the game's development. He doesn't clarify exactly how far along Project Ethos is on that track.

It's also not clear exactly how many employees are losing their jobs; Brinkman says 31st Union is "parting ways with some valued colleagues", but he doesn't specify the number of staffers being let go.

I'm a little perplexed at the contradictory messaging being employed here, as I'm sure you will be as well. It's hard for me to fathom how studio leadership can be "more confident" in Project Ethos than ever before while cutting back staff numbers and reworking the game's direction.

Honestly, given how hostile the current climate seems to be when it comes to live-service games competing for your time and money, I wouldn't be surprised if Project Ethos' days are numbered. This one may never see the light of day, or it could limp along for a couple of weeks before going the way of Concord, Highguard, and many other live-service contenders.

If you're wondering exactly what Project Ethos is, well, you're almost certainly not alone. 2K revealed the game as a roguelike hero shooter back in late 2024, and the resounding "meh" with which it was seemingly met reportedly resulted in the firing of 31st Union's then-studio head.

Project Ethos has maintained radio silence since early 2025. It's not particularly encouraging to think that the game has now lost some of the staff working on it, but perhaps the remaining developers can pull together and make this one something special. You can wishlist it on Steam if you want to keep abreast of its progress.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/project-ethos-layoffs-31st-union
